title: A Tale of Two Beasts
author: Fiona Roberton
description: A wonderful story about how two different characters view the exact same situation from completely different perspectives.

sentences.englishOriginal: I was walking home from Grandma's house, through the deep dark woods, when I spied a strange little beast.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當我從外婆家走路回家，穿過那片深邃黑暗的樹林時，突然瞧見了一隻奇怪的小野獸。

vocabularyNotes.title: spy
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「spy」在這裡當作動詞，表示「突然看見」或「發現」，通常指看見不容易發現的人或物。
examples: He spied a rare bird hiding among the branches.

grammarNotes.title: was/were + V-ing... when + past tense
grammarNotes.explanation: 這個句型用來表示「某件事正在發生時，突然被另一件突發的事情打斷」。
examples: I was watching TV when the phone rang.

sentences.englishOriginal: He was stuck up a tree, and whining sadly so I decided to rescue him.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他被困在一棵樹上，哀怨地嗚嗚哭泣著，於是我決定拯救他。

vocabularyNotes.title: whine
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「whine」指的是「發出哀鳴聲」或「哼哼唧唧地哭著」，也可以用來指人抱怨、發牢騷。
examples: The puppy whined at the door until we let him in.

phraseNotes.title: stuck up a tree
phraseNotes.explanation: 「stuck」是 stick 的過去分詞，在這裡當形容詞表示「被困住的」。「stuck up a tree」就是固定用來形容貓狗或小動物困在樹上動彈不得的道地說法。
examples: The poor cat has been stuck up a tree all morning.

sentences.englishOriginal: I will call you Fang! I told him, and I wrapped him warmly up in my scarf, and carried him safely home.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我要叫你小尖牙！」我對他說，接著用我的圍巾把他暖呼呼地包起來，安全地帶回了家。

phraseNotes.title: wrap up
phraseNotes.explanation: 「wrap up」意思是「包裹好」或「穿得暖和」。這裡中間加入了名詞代名詞，寫成「wrapped him up」。
examples: Make sure you wrap up warm before going outside into the snow.

sentences.englishOriginal: I gave him a lovely bath, and a gorgeous new hat and sweater, and a delicious bowl of fresh nuts.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我給他洗了個舒服的熱水澡，換上一頂超漂亮的全新帽子和毛衣，還給了他一碗美味的新鮮堅果。

vocabularyNotes.title: gorgeous
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「gorgeous」是一個非常常用且生動的形容詞，用來讚美事物「極其美麗、極其漂亮」或「令人賞心悅目的」。
examples: You look absolutely gorgeous in that dress!

sentences.englishOriginal: I made him a beautiful house, and gave him Lord Rex to play with.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我幫他蓋了一座漂亮的房子，還把雷克斯勛爵送給他當玩伴。

grammarNotes.title: to play with (Infinitive phrase modifying noun)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「to play with」是不定詞片語，當作形容詞用來修飾前面的名詞 Lord Rex（雷克斯勛爵），表示「用來一起玩的對象」。介係詞 with 不可省略。
examples: The children have plenty of toys to play with.

sentences.englishOriginal: I took him out for lots of long walkies to keep him fit and healthy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我帶他出去散了好多好長好長的步，好讓他保持強壯和健康。

vocabularyNotes.title: walkies
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「walkies」是英國與英文母語者在對寵物（尤其是狗）說話時，所使用的幼稚、可愛化童言童語，意思就是「散步」。
examples: Who wants to go for walkies?

sentences.englishOriginal: And I showed him off to all of my friends, who loved him nearly as much as I did.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我還向我所有的朋友炫耀他，他們對他的喜愛簡直快要跟我一樣多了。

phraseNotes.title: show off
phraseNotes.explanation: 「show off」意思是「炫耀」或「賣弄」，這裡指的是興高采烈地展示自己的新寵物給別人看。
examples: She loved showing off her engagement ring to her colleagues.

sentences.englishOriginal: But for some strange reason, the little beast did not look very happy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但不知道為了什麼奇怪的原因，這隻小野獸看起來一點也不開心。

phraseNotes.title: for some strange reason
phraseNotes.explanation: 這個固定片語非常好用，意思是「由於某種奇怪的原因」或「不知怎麼搞的」，用來表達令人不解的現象。
examples: For some strange reason, the computer won't turn on today.

sentences.englishOriginal: In fact, he was looking rather hot.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 事實上，他看起來反而顯得相當燥熱。

vocabularyNotes.title: rather
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「rather」在這裡作為副詞，用來修飾形容詞，意思是「相當、頗為」，語氣通常比 quite 再稍微強烈或肯定一點。
examples: The test was rather difficult, but she passed it.

sentences.englishOriginal: I hope he's not sick, I thought, and opened the window to cool him down.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「希望他不是生病了，」我心想，於是把窗戶打開好讓他涼快一下。

phraseNotes.title: cool down
phraseNotes.explanation: 「cool down」意思是「變涼快」或「降溫」，在日常對話中，也可以用來指人「冷靜下來」。
examples: A cold drink will help you cool down on a hot summer afternoon.

sentences.englishOriginal: But then something terrible happened! He threw off his clothes, leapt out of the window, and ran away as fast as he could, back to the deep dark woods.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但就在那時，可怕的事情發生了！他一把扯掉身上的衣服，跳出窗外，使出渾身解數沒命似地逃跑，一溜煙逃回了那片深邃黑暗的樹林裡。

vocabularyNotes.title: leap
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「leap」是「跳躍」的意思（過去式為 leapt 或 leaped），通常指大幅度地跳過或跳出某個地方，比普通的 jump 更有畫面感。
examples: The deer leapt over the wooden fence effortlessly.

phraseNotes.title: throw off
phraseNotes.explanation: 「throw off」在這裡指的是極其迅速且粗魯地「脫掉、扯掉」身上的衣物。
examples: As soon as he got home, he threw off his heavy coat.

phraseNotes.title: as fast as one could
phraseNotes.explanation: 這個固定結構意思是「盡其所能地快」，用來形容竭盡全力的速度。
examples: She ran to the bus stop as fast as she could.

sentences.englishOriginal: I wanted to go look for him, but Mama had other plans.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我很想出去找他，但媽媽早就幫我安排了別的計畫（要做別的事）。

phraseNotes.title: have other plans
phraseNotes.explanation: 「have other plans」字面意思是「有其他計畫」，在生活中常被用來當作幽默或委婉的說法，表示「有些事（如家事、義務）不讓你如願做你想做的事」。
examples: I wanted to sleep in on Saturday, but my parents had other plans.

sentences.englishOriginal: I couldn't sleep. I missed the little beast, and wondered if I would ever see him again.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我翻來覆去睡不著。我好想念那隻小野獸，心裡直想著不知道還有沒有機會再見到他。

sentences.englishOriginal: But then a small furry shadow appeared at the foot of my bed. The strange little beast had returned.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但接著，一個小小的毛茸茸黑影出現在我的床尾。那隻奇怪的小野獸居然回來了。

phraseNotes.title: at the foot of the bed
phraseNotes.explanation: 英文中會用「the foot of the bed」來指「床尾（腳睡的那一頭）」，與之相對的是「the head of the bed（床頭）」。
examples: Our dog always sleeps at the foot of my bed.

grammarNotes.title: Past Perfect Tense (過去完成式)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「had returned」是過去完成式。在「影子出現（過去式 appeared）」的那個當下之前，這隻小野獸就已經回到了房間內，因此用過去完成式來強調動作發生的先後順序。
examples: When I arrived at the station, the train had already left.

sentences.englishOriginal: He seemed quite pleased to see me, and I began to think that maybe, just maybe... he wasn't that strange after all.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他看起來見到我挺高興的，而我也開始覺得，或許，就只是或許……他其實也沒那麼奇怪嘛。

phraseNotes.title: after all
phraseNotes.explanation: 「after all」意思是「畢竟」或「終究、其實」，經常用來表示事情的結果與先前預期的並不一樣。
examples: It didn't rain today, so we had our picnic after all.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: maybe, just maybe...
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 這是老外講故事或聊天時很愛用的一種修辭手法，重複「just maybe」來拉長語氣，營造出一種帶有期待、奇妙或心軟的戲劇張力。
examples: If we practice every day, maybe, just maybe, we can win the championship.

sentences.englishOriginal: A Tale of Two Beasts, Part Two: The Terrible Beast.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 兩隻野獸的故事，第二部分：那隻可怕的怪獸。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was hanging from my favorite tree, singing happily to the birds when I was ambushed by a terrible beast!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我正倒掛在我最喜歡的那棵樹上，開心地唱歌給小鳥聽，結果……我竟然被一隻可怕的怪獸給伏擊了！

vocabularyNotes.title: ambush
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「ambush」意思是「伏擊」或「埋伏偷襲」。這裡使用的是過去被動態（was ambushed），生動地描繪出野獸突然遭到人類小女孩抓住的震驚感受。
examples: The soldiers were ambushed while marching through the canyon.

sentences.englishOriginal: She growled at me, and tied me up, and carried me off to her secret lair.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她對著我低聲吼叫，把我全身綁了起來，然後把我一路擄回了她的祕密巢穴。

vocabularyNotes.title: lair
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「lair」原指「野獸的巢穴」或「躲藏處」。從小動物的角度來看，人類小女孩的家就像是一個神祕又可怕的惡魔祕密基地。
examples: The fox retreated into its underground lair.

phraseNotes.title: tie up
phraseNotes.explanation: 「tie up」意思是「綑綁起來」或「綁緊」。這裡代名詞置於中間寫成「tied me up」。
examples: We tied up the packages with strong string.

phraseNotes.title: carry off
phraseNotes.explanation: 「carry off」在此指強行「帶走」或「擄走」某人或某物。
examples: The eagle swooped down and carried off the rabbit.

sentences.englishOriginal: She made me disgustingly clean, and dressed me up in a ridiculous hat and sweater, and tried to make me eat squirrel food.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她把我洗得乾淨到令人作嘔（噁心死了！），還強迫我穿上蠢到爆的帽子和毛衣，甚至還打算逼我吃松鼠才吃的食物。噁！

vocabularyNotes.title: disgustingly
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「disgustingly」是副詞，源自於 disgust。字面意思是「令人噁心地」，但在日常生活中，常被用來當作一種誇張的修飾語，表示「極度、到了讓人受不了的地步」。
examples: He is disgustingly rich, yet he never tips the waiters.

vocabularyNotes.title: ridiculous
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「ridiculous」意思是「荒謬的、可笑的」或「愚蠢的」。
examples: It's ridiculous to go out in this heavy rain without an umbrella.

phraseNotes.title: dress up
phraseNotes.explanation: 「dress up」通常指「精心打扮」或「穿上盛裝/變裝」。這裡指被小女孩強行套上衣服當成洋娃娃打扮。
examples: Kids love to dress up in costumes for Halloween.

grammarNotes.title: make / try to make someone do something
grammarNotes.explanation: 「make」在此是使役動詞，後面接受詞後，必須接「原形動詞」（如 clean、eat）。表示「強迫某人做某事」。
examples: Our coach made us run ten laps around the field.

sentences.englishOriginal: She kept me in a tiny box, with nothing for me to do and nowhere for me to hang from.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她把我關在一個小小的盒子裡，在那裡我無事可做，也找不到任何可以讓我倒掛的地方。

grammarNotes.title: nothing / nowhere for someone to do / hang from
grammarNotes.explanation: 「nothing for me to do」表示「沒有任何事情可以讓我去做的」；「nowhere for me to hang from」則指「沒有任何地方可以供我懸掛」。介係詞 from 在此絕對不可省略，因為小動物原本是「hang from a tree（從樹上倒掛下來）」。
examples: There was nowhere for us to sit down in the crowded room.

sentences.englishOriginal: She made me walk backwards, and forwards, and backwards again, for no reason whatsoever.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她還逼著我往後走、往前走、再往後走，根本就莫名其妙，完全沒有任何理由！

vocabularyNotes.title: whatsoever
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「whatsoever」可用作副詞或形容詞，用法相當於 whatever，但語氣強烈得多。通常放在名詞（如 reason）後面，與否定的語境連用（如 for no reason），表示「絲毫、不管怎樣的……都完全沒有」。
examples: I have no interest whatsoever in fashion.

phraseNotes.title: for no reason
phraseNotes.explanation: 非常實用的對話片語，意思是「毫無理由地」或「平白無故地」。
examples: She started crying for no reason at all.

sentences.englishOriginal: She showed me off to a herd of even wilder beasts, who were just as terrible as she was.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她把我拿去向一群更野蠻的怪獸炫耀，那些傢伙跟她一樣可怕！

vocabularyNotes.title: herd
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「herd」通常指牛群、羊群或馬群等「獸群」。在故事中，小動物用這個詞來形容圍過來大呼小叫的人類死小鬼們，充滿了幽默的諷刺感。
examples: A herd of cattle was blocking the narrow country road.

grammarNotes.title: just as + adj + as...
grammarNotes.explanation: 這個同等比較級句型意思是「跟……完全一模一樣地……」。結尾的 she was 也可以直接簡寫為 her。
examples: He is just as tall as his older brother.

sentences.englishOriginal: I had had enough! I made a cunning plan, and put it straight into action.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我受夠了！我擬定了一個非常狡猾的妙計，而且立刻付諸實行。

vocabularyNotes.title: cunning
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「cunning」意思是「狡猾的」或「精明的」，有時在童話或笑話中也可以用來指「聰明、巧妙的（帶點惡作劇成分）」。
examples: The fox used a cunning trick to escape from the hunter.

phraseNotes.title: have had enough
phraseNotes.explanation: 「have had enough」是一個常見口語，意思是「受夠了、忍無可忍了」。第一個 had 是助動詞，第二個 had 是動詞的過去分詞。
examples: I've had enough of your excuses!

phraseNotes.title: put something into action
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「將某事（計畫、想法）付諸實行」或「採取行動」。中間加入了 straight，表示「立刻、馬上」。
examples: It's time to put our new ideas into action.

sentences.englishOriginal: I raced back to the deep dark woods, before the terrible beast could catch me. Free once more!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在那隻可怕的怪獸抓到我之前，我拼命狂奔回那片深邃黑暗的樹林。我又自由了！

phraseNotes.title: once more
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「再一次」或「重新」，相當於 once again 或 again。
examples: He wanted to see her once more before she left the country.

sentences.englishOriginal: It was peaceful in the deep dark woods, a bit too peaceful perhaps. Silence. And also, a bit wet.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這片深邃黑暗的樹林裡原本很平靜……不過，或許有點平靜得過頭了。一片死寂。而且，還有點濕漉漉的。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: a bit too... perhaps
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 日常口語中很實用的含蓄修飾法，把「有點太……了」後面加上「perhaps（或許）」，能讓語氣帶有一種微妙的動搖感或反思意味。
examples: The soup is a bit too salty, perhaps.

sentences.englishOriginal: In weather like this one could do with a nice warm hat. I snuck back to retrieve it under cover of darkness.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在這種鬼天氣裡，要是能有一頂暖呼呼的漂亮帽子該有多好。於是我趁著夜色的掩護，偷偷溜回去打算把帽子拿回來。

vocabularyNotes.title: sneak
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「sneak」意思是「偷偷地走」或「潛入」（過去式常寫作 snuck 或 sneaked）。
examples: We snuck into the movie theater after it had already started.

vocabularyNotes.title: retrieve
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「retrieve」是一個比較正式的動詞，意思是「取回」或「重新找回（已經失去或遺留在那裡的東西）」。
examples: The dog jumped into the lake to retrieve the ball.

phraseNotes.title: could do with something
phraseNotes.explanation: 這是極其道地的英式/生活英文口語短語，意思是「很需要某物」或「如果有某物的話會大有幫助」。
examples: I'm exhausted; I could really do with a cup of coffee right now.

phraseNotes.title: under cover of darkness
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「趁著夜色的掩護」或「在黑暗的遮蔽下」，常用來形容祕密、不為人知的夜間行動。
examples: The thieves escaped from the house under cover of darkness.

sentences.englishOriginal: The terrible beast was waiting for me. She seemed quite pleased to see me, and I began to think that maybe, just maybe... she wasn't that terrible after all.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 結果，那隻可怕的怪獸竟然一直在等著我。她看起來見到我挺高興的，而我也開始覺得，或許，就只是或許……她其實也沒那麼可怕嘛。

sentences.englishOriginal: The way you see something is completely different from the way your friend sees something, or your mom sees something.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你看待某件事的角度，會和你朋友看待它的角度、或是你媽媽看待它的角度完全不一樣。

phraseNotes.title: different from
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「與……不同」，後面直接加上比較的對象。口語中偶爾會聽到 different than，但 different from 是最正統、最標準的用法。
examples: Life in the city is very different from life in the countryside.

grammarNotes.title: The way + subject + verb (Noun clause as subject)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「The way you see something」字面意思是「你看到某物的方式/方法」，在這裡當作整句話的主詞，指代「你看事情的角度、觀點」。
examples: I love the way she smiles.

sentences.englishOriginal: And to get to see both characters' point of view is cool.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而能夠同時看到兩個角色的觀點，真的是一件很酷的事情。

phraseNotes.title: point of view
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「觀點」或「看法、視角」，在日常對話與寫作中都極其常用，常縮寫為 POV。
examples: From my point of view, this plan is too risky.

phraseNotes.title: get to do something
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語中很普遍的片語，意思是「有機會能夠去做某事」或「得以做某事」，通常帶有幸運、開心的語感。
examples: We got to meet the famous actor after his performance.

sentences.englishOriginal: Make sure you check out all of our stories, and keep watching, and keep reading.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 請務必去看看我們所有的故事，並且保持觀看、保持閱讀喔！

phraseNotes.title: make sure
phraseNotes.explanation: 意思是「務必、確保」，後面通常引導一個省略了 that 的子句，用來叮嚀、提醒對方某件事。
examples: Make sure you lock the door before going to bed.

phraseNotes.title: check out
phraseNotes.explanation: 這裡當作口語用法，意思是「去看看、去瞧瞧」某些有趣的人事物。
examples: You should check out that new cafe down the street; it's really good.

grammarNotes.title: keep + V-ing
grammarNotes.explanation: 「keep」後面接動詞的 V-ing 形式（動名詞），用來表示「持續不斷地做某個動作」。
examples: Just keep swimming, and you will get there eventually.
